pattern: A valid subsequence either consists entirely of even or odd numbers, or alternates between even and odd — check all three cases.
hints: The consecutive sum condition (sub[i]+sub[i+1]) mod 2 must be constant — so either all sums are even (all same parity) or all sums are odd (alternating parity).

Case 1: all elements have the same parity — count all evens or all odds and take the larger. Case 2: elements alternate parity — greedily extend by taking any element whose parity differs from the last chosen.
The alternating case is a linear scan: keep track of the expected next parity and extend whenever the current element matches it.

edge_inputs.description: all elements the same value — same parity, full length
two elements, one odd one even — alternating length 2 wins
minimum length — two elements of same parity
more odds than evens — all-odds case vs alternating
only one even among many odds

stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    return [[rng.randint(1, 10**7) for _ in range(max(2, size))]]
